bead.title: Repair FEAT-004 and label dated sibling-flow docs
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree. · ADR-005 Concern practices.md is HELIX-activity-keyed — Every concern's practices.md organizes its per-activity content under HELIX activity headings: Discover, Frame, Design, Test, Build, Deploy, Iterate. Topic-keyed or quality-gates-keyed organization is no longer acceptable for practices.md. Why: Per-activity context-digest assembly must be a deterministic lookup, not a heuristic. The concerns library must use one consistent organizing principle so that any consumer — context assembler, alignment review, methodology docs — extracts the practices for a given activity by reading the corresponding heading.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Repair docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-plugin-packaging.md and add bounded historical/report/plan dispositions to dated docs that still present retired sibling-flow architecture as current. FEAT-004 has known blind relabel corruption and abandoned 17-skill plus /helix:<x> selector language. Reconstruct intent around one public helix skill and plugin packaging without inventing implementation status. Dated family/library/sibling/result docs under docs/helix/02-design should be marked historical/report/plan when they teach retired sibling architecture as current. In scope: FEAT-004 and targeted dated source docs. Out of scope: website mirrors except if mechanically regenerated by a later bead, SKILL.md, fixtures.
bead.acceptance:
1. rg -n "17-skill|/helix:<|helix-data|helix-infra|helix-web|~/.local/the HELIX skill" docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-plugin-packaging.md returns no current-doctrine hits.
2. rg -n "historical|superseded|report|plan" docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-06-03-* docs/helix/02-design/*results* docs/helix/02-design/*family* docs/helix/02-design/*linkage* shows dispositions on targeted dated docs that otherwise read as current.
3. Changed governed docs are stamped with ddx doc stamp <changed paths> after review, not ddx doc stamp --all.
4. ddx doc stale --json exits 0 and remaining stale docs are recorded.
5. git diff --check passes.
